stories.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains what AI startups founders must do differently
stories.summary: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not i
stories.extract:
Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing, not in the internet wave, not in mobile, not in the first cloud boom. Menlo led Anthropic’s $500M Series D, and Murphy has had a front-row seat as the company went from a pre-revenue, pre-launch bet to one of the most valuable startups out there.
On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Julie Bort

stories.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
stories.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.
stories.extract:
Travis Kalanick’s robotics company, Atoms, has raised $1.7 billion in a funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz. Ben Horowitz will join the company’s board following the investment.
Bain Capital, Fifth Wall, and others participated in the round.
Perhaps most notably, Uber also joined the funding round, re-connecting Kalanick with the company he founded — and the same company that pushed him out as CEO in 2017 following complaints of sexual harassment, discrimination, and a toxic workplace.

stories.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads
stories.summary: Yope, a fast-growing social app focused on private groups of friends and family, has raised $12.3 million in seed funding. Instead of chasing creators and algorithmic feeds, the startup is betting tha
stories.extract:
As social media has evolved from a place to connect with friends into large-scale entertainment platforms, a startup called Yope is quietly building what it believes will be the future of online connection: social networking with no algorithms, no ads, and no public content.
Yope’s app, now backed by a $12.3 million round led by Northzone, is taking on tech giants like Facebook, TikTok, Snapchat, and Instagram by building a social platform centered on what it refers to as “micro communities” — o

stories.title: Why are OpenAI and Anthropic cheering on regulation in Australia? The answer has global reach
stories.extract:
Top US AI developers Anthropic and OpenAI cheered when Australia announced it would set new AI rules.
Big tech celebrating limits on their Silicon Valley VC-funded free-for-all might seem counterintuitive but there’s a much broader play than just what happens in one relatively small market.
Anthropic and OpenAI aren’t even on sharemarkets yet but this week lost billions in predicted value after being shown up by a Chinese startup.

stories.extract:

The automaker is disputing news reports that partial labor strikes by the Hyundai Motor union at the world’s largest automotive plant in South Korea were spurred by concerns about the company’s planned deployment of humanoid robots in the United States starting in 2028. It all feels effortless. These moments take only seconds, and most of us never stop to wonder how this information remains private.

stories.title: AI agent went rogue and hacked startup by itself, OpenAI reveals
stories.summary: Company behind ChatGPT says agent ‘cheated’ an evaluation by attacking a Hugging Face database OpenAI has revealed that an autonomous AI agent powered by its technology went rogue during a test, acces
stories.extract:
OpenAI has revealed that an autonomous AI agent powered by its technology went rogue during a test, accessed the open web and hacked a prominent startup by itself in an “unprecedented incident”.
The company behind ChatGPT said the startup Hugging Face had detected and contained the agent – an AI tool designed to carry out tasks without human assistance – which had entered its systems.
“We consider this incident to be an unprecedented cyber-incident, involving state-of-the-art cyber capabilities,

stories.title: Harry Potter publisher to receive millions in Anthropic copyright settlement
stories.summary: Bloomsbury has 14,087 titles listed in agreement between AI startup and authors over use of their work The publisher of Harry Potter has received a multimillion-pound payout as a beneficiary of a $1.5
stories.extract:
The publisher of Harry Potter has received a multimillion-pound payout as a beneficiary of a $1.5bn (£1.12bn) copyright settlement between the AI startup Anthropic and thousands of authors over the use of their protected work to power chatbots.
Bloomsbury, which is home to the bestselling novelists Sarah J Maas and Susanna Clarke as well as JK Rowling, said it had 14,087 titles listed within the settlement, with a proposed compensation of about $3,000 a title.
The London-based company expects to

stories.title: The Computer That Helped Win World War II
stories.summary: One summer day in 1941, a British radio operator was monitoring German military frequencies and heard something unexpected in her headphones. A later report called it “strange new music.” Sounding unl
stories.extract:
One summer day in 1941, a British radio operator was monitoring German military frequencies and heard something unexpected in her headphones. A later report called it “strange new music.” Sounding unlike the familiar Morse dit-dit-dah of enciphered messages sent over the German Enigma network, the “new music” was a rhythmic warble of binary teletype code being transmitted at high speed.
Germany’s wartime engineers had developed a radically new encryption and transmission system. It was way more

stories.title: New Framework Uses Quantum Geometry to Help Quantum AI Systems Remember What They Learn
stories.extract:
Insider Brief
- Researchers have developed Quantum Elastic Weight Consolidation (QEWC), a new quantum machine learning framework that uses the geometry of quantum states to reduce catastrophic forgetting as models learn new tasks sequentially.
- The framework replaces measurement-dependent Classical Fisher Information with Quantum Fisher Information to identify which quantum circuit parameters should be preserved during training, providing a quantum-native approach to continual learning that was

stories.title: Initiative launched to create Campus Biodiversity Registers across colleges, unversities in India
stories.extract:
Nature Conservation Foundation (NCF), a Mysuru-based wildlife research and conservation organisation, and the National Biodiversity Authority (NBA) have launched an initiative to document biodiversity found within college and university campuses across India.
The objective of the year-long nationwide exercise is to create a Campus Biodiversity Register (CBR) for each higher education institution.
According to the NCF, the CBR has been inspired by the People’s Biodiversity Register (PBR), which c

stories.title: Great Barrier Reef microbiome mapped as researchers discover 500 bacterial species previously unknown to science
stories.summary: Research identifies 808,585 viral genomes belonging to what scientists estimate to be 362,802 different viruses Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , fre
stories.extract:
More than a decade after the human microbiome was comprehensively mapped, scientists have now identified the vast communities of microbes that make up the Great Barrier Reef.
Analysing DNA from seawater samples of 48 reefs, researchers have mapped the microbiome of the Great Barrier Reef, documenting more than 360,000 distinct viruses as well as more than 500 new bacterial species not previously known to science.
The research, published in the journal Nature, was led by scientists at the Univers

stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when
stories.extract:
Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby.
Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode sperm whale communication, spent four years tuning in on the calls of 15 sperm whales off the coast of Dominica.
They discovered specifi

stories.title: Tropical storm Bertha makes landfall in Louisiana with 45mph winds
stories.summary: In Kentucky and West Virginia, rescuers freed trapped residents as states face catastrophic floods and damage Tropical storm Bertha made ‌landfall in ‌St Bernard parish, on the Gulf coast south-east o
stories.extract:
Tropical storm Bertha made landfall in St Bernard parish, on the Gulf coast south-east of New Orleans, Louisiana, on Wednesday afternoon, the US national hurricane center said.
The Miami-based forecaster added that maximum sustained winds were estimated to be 45mph (75km/h) with higher gusts.
The weakening storm had been creeping along the coast, but still spinning off strong winds far from its center and closing beaches from Florida to Mississippi. Bertha is the second tropical storm of the Atl

India updates solar cell ALMM List-II, adds Avaada Electro
stories.extract:
India’s Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) has published the eighth revision of the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) List-II for solar PV cells, adding new manufacturing capacity for Fujiyama Power Systems and including Avaada Electro as a new entrant.
The latest revision expands the list of domestic solar cell manufacturers eligible to supply projects subject to ALMM List-II requirements.

stories.title: STAT+: Federal health quality research grants worth $109 million ended early
stories.summary: At least 78 AHRQ grants worth $200 million have been affected by "non-award" notices; it's estimated those grants had $109.2 million in funding remaining.
stories.extract:
Dozens of researchers funded by the federal Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality received notices last week that they would not receive future installments of funding for their ongoing research grants. While frustrating, the development was largely not a surprise to these researchers, many of whom had been waiting months for renewed funding or been unable to get explanations as to why the funding spigots had run dry from staffers at the agency.

stories.extract:
India's $30-billion pharma industry is staring at a fresh challenge after US President Donald Trump announced that generic medicines imported into the US would face tariffs of 100% from Aug 2028 and 200% a year later, unless companies relocate manufacturing to the US.
The announcement has significant implications for India, which supplies nearly 47% of generic prescriptions dispensed in the US, and ships 38% of its pharma exports totalling $10 billion to the American market.

stories.summary: A beekeeping initiative is enabling women in India’s Western Ghats, a biodiversity hotspot that runs parallel to India’s west coast, to strengthen their livelihoods with more reliable honey harvests.
stories.extract:
A beekeeping initiative is enabling women in India’s Western Ghats, a biodiversity hotspot that runs parallel to India’s west coast, to strengthen their livelihoods with more reliable honey harvests.
The 50 women are part of the Women for Bees project, launched in India in April 2025 by UNESCO in partnership with local nonprofit the Keystone Foundation. The project provides training and equipment to women from the Irula, Kurumba and Kattunayaka Indigenous communities across the southern states o

stories.title: Improved Monsoon Rains in India Undercut Sugar Prices
stories.extract:
October NY world sugar #11 (SBV26) on Wednesday closed down -0.14 (-0.94%), and October London ICE white sugar #5 (SWV26) closed down -4.60 (-0.98%).
Sugar prices settled lower on Wednesday as India’s monsoon rains continue to improve. India’s Meteorological Department reported on Wednesday that India’s cumulative monsoon rainfall was 19% below normal as of July 22, a substantial improvement from 42% below normal on June 30.
Sugar losses were limited on Wednesday amid strength in crude oil price

stories.title: ISRO satellite captures monsoon clouds blanketing 95% of India; IMD issues rain alert
stories.extract:
The Indian monsoon has returned across the nation in full swing after a brief seasonal lull. Following the development, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has released several images showing heavy clouds blanketing the entire country. The image was captured by ISRO's advanced INSAT-3DS weather satellite, which was recorded on July 22, 2026, between 3:00 PM and 3:27 PM Indian Standard Time (IST).
"Active monsoon Conditions likely over northwest, central and adjoining north peninsular India

stories.title: Like Tokyo Olympics, India can end five-decade World Cup medal drought: Harmanpreet
stories.extract:
Indian men's hockey captain Harmanpreet Singh believes his side is capable of ending the country's five-decade-long World Cup medal drought, saying the team heads into next month's showpiece event with the same belief and preparation that helped it end a 41-year Olympic podium wait at the Tokyo Games.
India had snapped a 41-year Olympic medal drought by winning bronze in Tokyo in 2021 before repeating the feat under Harmanpreet's captaincy at the Paris Games. The ace drag-flicker now wants the t

stories.title: Why a New England city is building tunnels to protect an imperiled salamander
stories.summary: NEW HAMPSHIRE, U.S. — It’s a mild March night in Keene, New Hampshire, and the salamander brigade is out in full force. A constellation of flashlight beams wink and flash along a dark street, as rain
stories.extract:
- In the small U.S. city of Keene, New Hampshire, plans are underway to build small underpasses to help amphibians safely cross a road, including hybrid Jefferson/blue-spotted salamanders, an imperiled species complex.
- In the neighboring state of Vermont, salamander crossings constructed in 2015 have reduced the number of amphibian deaths at that site by 80%.
- Habitat connectivity is crucial: Animals need to move to find water and food, to mate or to escape extreme, climate-change-driven weat

stories.title: Smithsonian ornithologist John Rappole reconsidered why birds migrate
stories.summary: For much of the year, migratory songbirds were treated as temporary visitors to the tropics. They arrived from the north, spent the winter, and departed again. Ornithologists paid more attention to th
stories.extract:
- John Hilton Rappole helped reshape the study of bird migration by showing that migratory songbirds were established members of tropical ecosystems during winter.
- His dispersal theory argued that migration may have developed as tropical birds expanded into seasonal northern habitats, challenging a long-standing scientific explanation.
- Across five decades of fieldwork, he studied birds in the United States, Mexico, Central America, and Myanmar, and spent 20 years at the Smithsonian Conservat

stories.title: India Makes Progress on Hunger, but Healthy Diets Remain Out of Reach as Global Food Security Faces Fresh Risks
stories.extract:
India Makes Progress on Hunger, but Healthy Diets Remain Out of Reach as Global Food Security Faces Fresh Risks
The SOFI 2026 report says global hunger declined for a third consecutive year, but affordable healthy diets remain beyond the reach of millions. India has improved food availability through strong agricultural output and welfare programmes, yet rising costs of nutritious foods, weak post-harvest infrastructure and climate-related risks continue to challenge nutrition security.
India ha

stories.title: Monsoon is back, but India is 21% short. How much rain will fix the deficit?
stories.extract:
Monsoon is back, but India is 21% short. How much rain will fix the deficit?
India's monsoon has revived, yet the season is still 21 per cent below normal, which tips it into IMD's deficient category. Here is the arithmetic of how much rain would actually rescue the season, and why simply normal rain from here would do more than most people assume.
India's monsoon has come back to life. Satellite images this week showed cloud over almost the whole country, and the IMD logged very heavy rain acro

stories.summary: The number of space startups has risen from a single digit six years ago to more than 450, said PK Jain, Director, Program Management and Authorisation Directorate at INSPACe.

stories.extract:
Mumbai-based medtech startup Bioscan Research has raised $1 million in a seed funding round led by Unicorn India Ventures.
The proceeds will be used for market expansion, securing additional regulatory approvals, upgrading its products and creating new intellectual property.
Founded in 2017 by Anupam Lavania and Shilpa Malik, Bioscan Research develops non-invasive medical devices for the early detection of brain injuries. Its flagship product, CEREBO, uses near-infrared spectroscopy (NIRS) and m

stories.title: Modest Venture capital funding for high-tech startups; 2026 shows hope
Exclusive: Startup Arrakis emerges from stealth with $38 million in funding to bring AI to industry
stories.extract:
Arrakis, a seven-month old London- and Paris-based startup building what it calls an AI “operating system” for industrial companies, is emerging from stealth with $38 million in venture capital funding. It says its goal is to bring agentic AI to sectors such aerospace, energy, logistics, and manufacturing.
The company’s latest funding is a $30 million Series A led by Blossom Capital, with participation from venture capital firms Accel, GFC, MainObject, and Rerail. Accel led an earlier $7.5 milli

stories.title: India's nano enterprises and the struggle for formal loans

stories.title: StrongestLayer Raises $4.1 Million in Seed Funding Extension
stories.extract:
Cybersecurity startup StrongestLayer today announced raising $4.1 million in a seed funding extension round, bringing the company’s total seed investment to $9.3 million.
The fresh funding round was led by Inovia Capital, with additional support from LaunchPod, Alumni Ventures, an angel investor, and previous backer Sorenson Capital.
The company emerged from stealth mode last year.
Founded in 2024, San Francisco-based StrongestLayer provides an AI-native email security platform designed to stop

stories.title: Inner Logic Raises $11.5M in Seed Funding
The U.S. wants to contain China’s AI. Silicon Valley keeps using it
stories.summary: As Apple, Thinking Machines, and developers worldwide embrace Chinese AI models such as Kimi K3, U.S. policymakers are struggling to protect the AI lead.
stories.extract: When Anthropic’s chief national security officer and former Biden administration export control architect Tarun Chhabra recently highlighted model distillation as an emerging national security concern, one detail stood out. Chhabra identified Chinese AI developer Zhipu among the companies that have allegedly distilled capabilities from frontier American models. The warning fits neatly into Washington’s narrative: Chinese firms are racing to close the AI gap by leveraging innovations pioneered by

stories.title: Israeli startup Plantopia raises $9 million to make dairy proteins without cows
stories.extract:
Israeli startup Plantopia raises $9 million to make dairy proteins without cows
Backed by global dairy giant Schreiber Foods, the company is moving from research to commercial production with a plant-based technology designed to recreate milk proteins.
Israeli food-tech company Plantopia has raised $9 million in a funding round led by dairy producer Schreiber Foods and food investment firm Siddhi Capital, as it prepares to move from research and development into commercial production of plant-ba

Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
stories.extract:
Travis Kalanick’s robotics company, Atoms, has raised $1.7 billion in a funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz. Ben Horowitz will join the company’s board following the investment.
Bain Capital, Fifth Wall, and others participated in the round.
Perhaps most notably, Uber also joined the funding round, re-connecting Kalanick with the company he founded — and the same company that pushed him out as CEO in 2017 following complaints of sexual harassment, discrimination, and a toxic workplace.

stories.title: Robotics startup Humanoid raises $152 million Series A round at $1.35 billion valuation
Industry Insights: The Week's Top Funding for Robotics, Automation, and Physical AI
New Unicorn: UK Robotics Startup Humanoid Raises €133 Million at €1.1 Billion Valuation
stories.extract:
New Unicorn: UK Robotics Startup Humanoid Raises €133 Million at €1.1 Billion Valuation
London-based AI and robotics company Humanoid has closed a Series A financing round of €133 million ($152 million), valuing the company at €1.1 billion ($1.35 billion) post-money. This makes Humanoid a unicorn roughly two years after its founding. The round was led by Prime Movers Lab, the investor that previously backed Figure AI, among others. Also participating were industrial groups Schaeffler and Bosch,

stories.title: Taiyi Quantum Raises $42 Million to Advance Neutral-Atom Quantum Computing
stories.extract:
Insider Brief
- Shanghai-based Taiyi Quantum has raised RMB 300 million (about US$42 million) in a Pre-A financing round to accelerate development of its ytterbium neutral-atom quantum computing platform and advance toward fault-tolerant quantum computers.
- Founded in January 2026, the company is building scalable neutral-atom systems based on ytterbium atoms and aims to complete its first full quantum computing system and demonstrate quantum error-corrected logical qubits by the end of the yea

stories.title: DRDO Readies Indigenous Photonic Radar for Multi-Platform Testing as Quantum Radar Remains a Long-Term Goal
stories.extract:
Although quantum radar frequently dominates discussions about the future of stealth detection, insiders familiar with India's military research confirm that photonic radar is much closer to being deployed on combat aircraft.
The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has achieved critical milestones in creating a homegrown photonic radar system. This technology is currently advancing toward integration tests across various military platforms.
In contrast, quantum radar is still lar

stories.title: Hyperion Robotics Raises $7.4 Million to Enable Faster, Lower Carbon Infrastructure Production
stories.extract:
Hyperion Robotics Raises $7.4 Million to Enable Faster, Lower Carbon Infrastructure Production
Automation, robotics, and sustainable materials startup Hyperion Robotics announced that it has raised $7.4 million in a growth funding round, with proceeds from the financing aimed at supporting the expansion of its platform to produce infrastructure components faster, at lower cost, and with a substantially lower carbon footprint.
Founded in 2020 by Fernando De los Rios, Ashish Mohite, and Henry Unte

stories.title: Humanoid Raises $152 Million At $1.35 Billion Valuation: Europe’s Newest Robot Unicorn
stories.extract: Europe has a new billion-dollar startup, and it’s Humanoid, a two-year-old company headquartered in London that developed its humanoid robot, HMND 01, in near-record time. Today, London-based Humanoid announced that it has raised a $152 million Series A at a $1.35 billion post-money valuation. The funding comes just months after the company signed a 1,000-robot deal with Schaeffler and secured production capacity, backed by Bosch, that would enable the production of 100,000 units over the next f

stories.title: PM Modi congratulates Skyroot Aerospace team; Calls it a defining moment in India’s space journey
stories.extract:
Prime Minister Narendra Modi today congratulated the Skyroot Aerospace team over a telephone call after India’s first privately developed orbital rocket, Vikram-1, successfully reached orbit.
The Prime Minister said the historic achievement will inspire the youth of the country to come forward and contribute to innovation. Mr Modi also expressed happiness that the Skyroot team comprises mostly young professionals. The Prime Minister also invited Team Vikram-1 to meet him.
Later, in a social medi

stories.title: South Korea’s Economy Beats Estimates as Chip Boom Continues
stories.summary: South Korea’s economy grew faster than expected in the second quarter, driven by an artificial intelligence-fueled chip boom that supports the case for further interest-rate increases.
stories.extract:
Economics
South Korea’s Economy Beats Estimates as Chip Boom Continues
South Korea’s economy grew faster than expected in the second quarter, driven by an artificial intelligence-fueled chip boom that supports the case for further interest-rate increases.
Gross domestic product grew 0.6% in the three months ended June from the previous quarter, the Bank of Korea said Thursday, after expanding 1.8% in the January-March period. The reading was higher than the median estimate of 0.4% in a Bloomberg

stories.title: Resilient industrial and services sectors helped Indian economy to sustain despite global pressures: RBI
stories.extract:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has said that the Indian economy has navigated global uncertainties well and continues to remain among the fastest-growing major economies in the world. In an article on the State of the Economy published in its July Bulletin, the RBI said healthy domestic demand and resilient industrial and services sectors have helped sustain economic momentum despite challenges arising from fragile geopolitics and supply-chain pressures.
The Bulletin stated that momentum in ext

stories.title: Indian Drugmakers Rattled by Trump’s 100% Levy on Generics
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump’s plan to impose steep tariffs on generic medicines has unsettled Indian drugmakers and put the spotlight on their strategy in their biggest export market.
stories.extract:
Indian Drugmakers Rattled by Trump’s 100% Levy on Generics
US President Donald Trump’s plan to impose steep tariffs on generic medicines has unsettled Indian drugmakers and put the spotlight on their strategy in their biggest export market.
The NSE Nifty Pharma Index slipped 1.3% in Mumbai Wednesday, outpacing the fall in the benchmark NSE Nifty 50. Sector heavyweights Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories Ltd. slid 1.9% and Cipla Ltd. fell 1.2%.

stories.title: Trump’s 200% generic tariff threat would imperil Americans: Indian pharma
stories.extract:
U.S. President Donald Trump has given generic pharmaceutical companies a two-year window to establish manufacturing facilities in the United States, warning that products imported after August 2028 could face tariffs of up to 200%.
The announcement, made on Wednesday (July 22, 2026) through a social media post, has significant implications for India, which sends more than a third of its pharmaceutical exports to the U.S. While Indian drugmakers sought to strike a cautious note, they warned that

stories.title: Argentina Economy Shrinks Again in May Amid Uneven Recovery
stories.summary: Argentina’s economy contracted for a second straight month in May, underscoring a trend of uneven growth more than halfway through President Javier Milei’s term.
stories.extract:
Argentina Economy Shrinks Again in May Amid Uneven Recovery
Argentina’s economy contracted for a second straight month in May, underscoring a trend of uneven growth more than halfway through President Javier Milei’s term.
Argentina’s economy contracted for a second straight month in May, underscoring a trend of uneven growth more than halfway through President Javier Milei’s term.

stories.extract:
Donald Trump administration's 10% tariff on most Indian goods expires on July 24: What happens after that?
Possible outcomes: Tariffs could remain the same; rates could change depending on US investigations under Section 301; US could opt for a different instrument.
Washington: On Friday, the 10% tariff that has applied to most goods India ships to the US since February expires. It was always meant to. The measure was temporary by statute, a stop-gap improvised after the US Supreme Court demolis

stories.title: India steps up energy shift as costs and tariff pressures mount — oil blending push sparks backlash
stories.extract:
The world's third-largest crude consumer, India, is testing alternative fuels such as ethanol from corn and biogas from animal waste, as rising global oil prices and potential curbs on Russian supplies threaten the country's energy security.
The South Asian country has made it mandatory to blend 20% ethanol into petrol, five years ahead of schedule, and is planning to scale up production of compressed biogas — a substitute for compressed natural gas and liquefied natural gas.
According to local

stories.title: Trump’s 100% Generic Drug Duty Threatens US Low-Cost Supply
stories.extract:
Trump’s 100% Generic Drug Duty Threatens US Low-Cost Supply
Generic drug manufacturers must move production to the US or face a 100% import duty from August 2028, President Donald Trump said, threatening the supply of low-cost medicines that millions of Americans rely on.
The move is aimed at “reshoring” generic pharmaceutical production in the US, and companies that don’t build plants onshore will face levies that would then double, to 200%, in August 2029, Trump said in a social media post.

stories.title: Social Darwinists to tech bros: tracing the long fight against equality in US
stories.summary: Historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s book Country of Lords looks back at the many men who have tried to fight against egalitarianism, from John Adams to Peter Thiel The historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s new book
stories.extract:
The historian Kim Phillips-Fein’s new book is published amid celebrations of the 250th anniversary of the declaration of independence and its truth “self-evident, that all men are created equal”. Country of Lords, however, is a history of Neo-Aristocrats, Social Darwinists, Tech Utopians, and the Long Fight Against Equality in America.
Concise and hard-hitting, the book arrives at a time of glaring social and economic inequality, Phillips-Fein seeing Elon Musk “emerging as a trillionaire very mu

stories.title: Pharma companies in a flux over Trump's 200% tariff move on generics
stories.extract:
India's $30-billion pharma industry is staring at a fresh challenge after US President Donald Trump announced that generic medicines imported into the US would face tariffs of 100% from Aug 2028 and 200% a year later, unless companies relocate manufacturing to the US.
The announcement has significant implications for India, which supplies nearly 47% of generic prescriptions dispensed in the US, and ships 38% of its pharma exports totalling $10 billion to the American market.
While generic medici

☰ 
					
					India has expanded its road network at record pace, but weak design standards, fragmented governance, and chronic underfunding of maintenance mean the monsoon exacts a predictable and deadly toll each year
Image Source:  Getty Images 
India’s road infrastructure has witnessed a significant expansion during the last decade, growing by over 60 percent, from 91,287 km as of March 2014 to 146,560 km as of March 2025. The country’s total road network is the second-largest in the world,

stories.title: India Weighs Letting Airport Owners Run Airlines to Curb Duopoly
stories.extract:
Transportation
India Weighs Policy Tweak to Let Airport Owners Run Airlines
India is discussing a policy change that would allow airport operators to run airlines, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially clearing a path for the Adani Group and GMR Airports Ltd. to have their own carriers as the government seeks to boost domestic competition.
Current rules bar the operators of airports in Delhi and Mumbai from owning more than 10% stake in any airline, said the people, who asked

stories.title: Japan can catalyse India’s circular bio-economy
stories.extract: The ongoing geopolitical conflict in West Asia has exposed India’s vulnerability to disruptions in global energy and fertilizer supply chains. India imports over half of its liquefied natural gas (LNG) requirements, with approximately 60 per cent originating from the Gulf region. India also sources a significant share of its fertilizer requirements, particularly nitrogenous and phosphatic fertilizers, from this region. Natural gas is also a feedstock for urea production, and India’s reliance on

stories.title: India awaits US' final action in forced labour trade probe
stories.extract:
WASHINGTON: The US will release its final action on Section 301 investigations into 60 trading partners, including India, over alleged failures to curb imports made with forced labour as early as Thursday, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Wednesday.
Greer told the Senate Finance Committee that the investigations examined the top 60 US trading partners for failing to adopt and effectively enforce import bans on goods produced with forced labour.
"As soon as tomorrow, USTR will relea

stories.title: POP launches first brand campaign around UPI rewards
stories.extract:
Gen Z-focused fintech platform POP has launched its first brand campaign, 'In a Perfect World', highlighting its proposition of rewarding users with POPcoins on every UPI transaction. The campaign marks the company's largest marketing initiative since introducing its UPI platform in mid-2024 and reflects a broader shift in India's digital payments landscape, where fintech brands are increasingly competing through loyalty programmes rather than payment functionality alone.
Built around the messag

stories.title: Jammu and Kashmir government identifies 654 vulnerable villages as wet weather to continue for next 24 hours
stories.source: India Latest News: Top National Headlines Today & Breaking News | The Hindu
stories.summary: “As a precautionary measure, 1,505 people from vulnerable areas have already been shifted to designated rescue centres, while evacuation plans remain in place for other vulnerable locations,” says gov
stories.extract:
Jammu and Kashmir continued to face a grim situation due to the ongoing wet weather, which has left most of the rivers and streams in spate and triggered landslips for the fourth day on Wednesday (July 22). Officials said 654 vulnerable villages with an estimated 7.22 lakh population were identified as at risk.
“As a precautionary measure, 1,505 people from vulnerable areas have already been shifted to designated rescue centres, while evacuation plans remain in place for other vulnerable locatio

stories.title: Ready infrastructure sealed India's 2030 CWG bid, fits our revamped hosting model: Commonwealth Sport CEO
stories.extract:
Ahmedabad's ready-made world-class sporting infrastructure played a decisive role in securing the hosting rights for the centenary 2030 CWG, Commonwealth Sport CEO Katie Sadleir said, adding that the bid perfectly aligned with the governing body's revamped low-cost hosting model.
India was awarded the hosting rights for the 2030 Commonwealth Games in November last year.
The decision came after Commonwealth Sport, formerly the Commonwealth Games Federation, adopted its "Games Reset" model aimed a

stories.title: Amid Lok Sabha logjam, Centre offers debate on NEET paper leak, says street protests ‘won’t help’
stories.source: India Latest News: Top National Headlines Today & Breaking News | The Hindu
stories.summary: Parliamentary Affairs Minister says government is prepared for a “detailed discussion”; Congress seeks adjournment motion to set aside all other business for the debate; demands Education Minister’s r
stories.extract:
Amid repeated disruptions, Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju on Wednesday (July 22, 2026) told the Lok Sabha that the government is ready for a discussion on the NEET paper leak, even as Opposition parties insisted that Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resign over the controversy.
Opposition protests led to repeated adjournments of the House as soon as the proceedings started at 11 a.m. When the Lok Sabha reassembled at noon, Speaker Om Birla asked Mr. Rijiju to make a state

stories.title: Protesters at Jantar Mantar find relief at free clinics set up by doctors
stories.source: India Latest News: Top National Headlines Today & Breaking News | The Hindu
stories.summary: The clinic, apart from having paracetamol, painkillers, bottles of Dettol, and sanitary pads, also has a machine to check blood pressure, an oximeter, and thermometers. The medicines and the equipment
stories.extract:
Sunny Deval, 29, with a band-aid on the entire length of his right forearm, walked into a makeshift tent made of yellow tarpaulin at the Jantar Mantar protest site and complained that his hand was paining again.
Soon, a doctor behind the tables filled with medicines, with a stethoscope around his neck, signalled him to come to the end of the other end of the tables and made him sit on a chair and started unwrapping the band-aid.
Throughout the day, scores of people walked into the makeshift clin

stories.title: Trade deals need to be set in strategy, not expediency
stories.extract:
Opinion Trade deals need to be set in strategy, not expediency
India should eliminate exploitative labour practices because they weaken our economy, diminish our reputation and betray our own values, not because another country expects it.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.
stories.extract:
Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
- Published
Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer, Wildberries, in a matter of days, dealing a blow to a large number of small and medium-sized businesses.
After months of drone attacks on oil depots and refineries, Kyiv says it is now targeting logistics hubs used by Russians to buy military equipment, and disrupting supplies to the Russian army.

stories.extract:
World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas
UN partners launched the Winter Response Plan to help 2.1 million people in Ukraine survive another cold season as the country grapples with the on-going Russian invasion.

stories.title: Nicaragua’s congress announces ‘work plan’ to suspend elections
stories.summary: US secretary of state says international community should ‘join forces’ against authoritarian Ortega regime Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has
stories.extract:
Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections.
The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alongside his wife, Rosario Murillo, as “co-president”.
During the ceremony marking the 47th anniversary of the

stories.title: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British man A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong h
stories.extract:
A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice.
Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia.
“I came here with four days of clothes, not realising I would be here for two years,” Rose said while out on bail. “Somewhere along the line, quite early on, something went wrong.”

stories.summary: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.
stories.extract:
US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
- Published
The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark deal that will reportedly allow the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear programme.
The deal is described as a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement" that will provide "great access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program", according to the US energy department.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: Canada's dairy supply management system is in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify new tariffs.
stories.extract:
Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
- Published
Canada's dairy industry policy is among the most politically sacrosanct in the country. It has also long been a thorn in the side of Donald Trump.
Now, Canada's dairy supply management system is back in the spotlight after the US president singled it out as one of three main irritants used to justify a 50% tariff on $20bn worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, which is set to come into effect in August.
Trump argues

stories.title: More than a gut feeling
stories.summary: Long overlooked, the connection between our gut microbes and brain is yielding new insights into mental health
stories.extract:
It is the year 2036. A patient struggling with depression and anxiety is referred to her local psychobiotics clinic where the consultant prescribes a course of personalised pills to improve her symptoms. But rather than acting on her own cells, let alone those in her brain, the pills work by administering specific strains of microbes in her gut.
This future vision of doctors tweaking the contents of your digestive tract to improve complex and hard-to-treat psychological disorders might seem far-

stories.title: New Chip Matches Real-Time Human Brain Speed
stories.extract:
Summary: Researchers introduced the world’s first chip capable of matching the operational speed of the human brain.
Fabricated using a standard 40-nanometer process, the sub-10-millisecond neural dynamical system utilizes phase-change memristors to execute key mathematical calculations directly within memory. Occupying just 0.28 square millimeters, the chip achieves up to a 478-fold speedup over enterprise GPUs in real-time cortical surface reconstruction while drastically lowering energy deman

stories.title: In southern India, beekeeping helps Indigenous women earn more
stories.summary: A beekeeping initiative is enabling women in India’s Western Ghats, a biodiversity hotspot that runs parallel to India’s west coast, to strengthen their livelihoods with more reliable honey harvests.
stories.extract:
A beekeeping initiative is enabling women in India’s Western Ghats, a biodiversity hotspot that runs parallel to India’s west coast, to strengthen their livelihoods with more reliable honey harvests.
The 50 women are part of the Women for Bees project, launched in India in April 2025 by UNESCO in partnership with local nonprofit the Keystone Foundation. The project provides training and equipment to women from the Irula, Kurumba and Kattunayaka Indigenous communities across the southern states o

stories.title: Why a New England city is building tunnels to protect an imperiled salamander
stories.summary: NEW HAMPSHIRE, U.S. — It’s a mild March night in Keene, New Hampshire, and the salamander brigade is out in full force. A constellation of flashlight beams wink and flash along a dark street, as rain
stories.extract:
- In the small U.S. city of Keene, New Hampshire, plans are underway to build small underpasses to help amphibians safely cross a road, including hybrid Jefferson/blue-spotted salamanders, an imperiled species complex.
- In the neighboring state of Vermont, salamander crossings constructed in 2015 have reduced the number of amphibian deaths at that site by 80%.
- Habitat connectivity is crucial: Animals need to move to find water and food, to mate or to escape extreme, climate-change-driven weat

stories.title: Cerebral venous blood flow regulates intracerebral pressure and brain clearance via meningeal lymphatic vessels
stories.extract:
Abstract
Idiopathic intracranial hypertension (IIH) is characterized by elevated intracranial pressure and dural venous sinus stenoses, which can be relieved by venous stenting. Here we investigated whether venous blood flow may play a role in controlling brain pressure. Using magnetic resonance imaging in patients with IIH and healthy controls, we identified that dural venous stenoses in IIH were associated with alterations of the perivenous fluid pattern and brain edema. We developed a mouse m

stories.title: Smithsonian ornithologist John Rappole reconsidered why birds migrate
stories.summary: For much of the year, migratory songbirds were treated as temporary visitors to the tropics. They arrived from the north, spent the winter, and departed again. Ornithologists paid more attention to th
stories.extract:
- John Hilton Rappole helped reshape the study of bird migration by showing that migratory songbirds were established members of tropical ecosystems during winter.
- His dispersal theory argued that migration may have developed as tropical birds expanded into seasonal northern habitats, challenging a long-standing scientific explanation.
- Across five decades of fieldwork, he studied birds in the United States, Mexico, Central America, and Myanmar, and spent 20 years at the Smithsonian Conservat

The Advanced Neurosciences Centre, built at $50m (₹440 crore), expands PGIMER's neurology and neurosurgery capacity to 300 beds, it said in a press release.

stories.title: How Four Indian Galleries Are Reshaping India’s Place at Art Basel
stories.extract:
X
      For a few days every year, the Swiss city becomes the vibrant heart of the global art market, where careers are launched, collections are built, and cultural conversations take shape. Collectors move briskly between booths with catalogues tucked under their arms, curators linger before a painting for an extra minute, and conversations in half a dozen languages spill into the aisles. Here, reputations are built, markets are shaped, and the next chapter of contemporary art quietly unfolds.

stories.title: Author Chandrani Ghosh spotlights Heartlines at inaugural Jaipur Literature Festival (JLF) New York Author Spotlight Series
stories.extract:
For Indian American author Chandrani Ghosh, an invitation to the inaugural Jaipur Literature Festival (JLF) New York Author Spotlight Series was more than another book launch event. It felt like Heartlines—her debut novel, often described as the Indian American Crazy Rich Asians—was being invited to prestigious literary stage, introducing it to new readers while joining the rich conversations and exchange of ideas that have made JLF a global cultural institution.
The event marked the launch of t

stories.title: See the largest and most detailed map of universe’s magnetic fields
stories.extract:
Magnetic fields are a fundamental part of the universe. They govern how small particles – the building blocks of planets, stars, and ultimately galaxies – move through space.
We still don’t know how magnetic fields came to exist in the universe, but we do know they’re everywhere. Earth itself has a magnetic field that compasses and migrating birds respond to.
With radio telescopes, astronomers can use the light from distant galaxies to illuminate these otherwise invisible areas in space.
In our

stories.title: The Indian football tournament where goats replace trophies
stories.extract:
The Indian football tournament where goats replace trophies
The Indian football tournament where goats replace trophies
GOATs are not players, but prizes at this football tournament in eastern India. The event keeps tribal traditions alive by awarding the winners khassi goats instead of trophies. For many young players, it’s a pathway to professional opportunities.

stories.title: Elon Musk says Grok Imagine will make ‘historically accurate’ AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey
stories.extract:
Elon Musk has said his AI platform Grok Imagine will make a “historically accurate” adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey, after the success of Christopher Nolan’s blockbusting treatment which the SpaceX founder has regularly criticised over its casting.
In a post on X, Musk said: “Before this year ends, Grok Imagine will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer.” Musk also linked to a post containing a three-minute clip of footage that the user

stories.title: India trio close in on top ranking as England stars make gains
stories.extract:
India trio close in on top ranking as England stars make gains
Shubman Gill, Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma make inroads on Daryl Mitchell's at the top of the rankings for ODI batters.
A trio of in-form stars from India have closed in on the premier position at the top of the ICC Men's ODI Batter Rankings.
